title: Slow Ember
description: F# minor, 136 BPM, F#m to D, 2-step drums, and a long lead that opens up in drop 2. The song is hand-held in detail, but the result should feel like a finished melodic garage record.
what_youll_learn: How i to bVI feels in F# minor over a 2-step groove
How to keep a lead emotional without letting it smear over the drums
How to use upper pad voicings and automation to keep a fast arrangement clear
How to make a second drop feel warmer and wider without changing the harmony

steps.title: Set the project
steps.instruction: Create a new Ableton Live set at 136 BPM and save it as 'original-13-slow-ember'. Make these tracks in order: DRUMS, BASS, PAD, LEAD, and a return track named VERB. The song is built from one two-chord motion, F# minor to D major, but the feeling should expand across the arrangement.
steps.why: Keeping the harmony simple lets the track grow through sound placement, automation, and lead timing instead of through unnecessary chord complexity.
steps.tip: This track should feel emotional, not busy.

steps.title: Load the 2-step drum kit
steps.instruction: Load a Drum Rack on DRUMS and place these exact samples: C1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/KICK/PH_UKG3_KICK_11.wav', D1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 2/One Shots/Clap/PH_UKG2_Clap_31.wav', F#1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/CLOSED/PH_UKG3_CLOSED_14.wav', G#1 = 'Production Hut- UK Garage Drums Volume 3/DRUMS ONE SHOTS/SHAKER/PH_UKG3_SHAKER_10.wav'.
steps.why: This kit has enough bite for 136 BPM 2-step without sounding abrasive when the lead comes in.
steps.tip: The clap is the fixed point in the groove. The kicks are the moving part.

steps.title: Program the 2-step drums
steps.instruction: Create a 2-bar DRUMS clip. All note lengths are 0.25 beats. KICK C1: 1.1.1 vel 97, 1.2.3 vel 84, 1.4.4 vel 76, 2.1.3 vel 88, 2.3.1 vel 82, 2.4.3 vel 78. CLAP D1: 1.2.1 vel 92, 1.4.1 vel 90, 2.2.1 vel 92, 2.4.1 vel 90. CLOSED HAT F#1: 1.1.3 vel 64, 1.2.4 vel 58, 1.3.3 vel 63, 1.4.3 vel 60, 2.1.3 vel 64, 2.2.4 vel 58, 2.3.3 vel 64, 2.4.3 vel 60. SHAKER G#1: 1.1.4 vel 42, 1.3.4 vel 40, 2.1.4 vel 42, 2.3.4 vel 40. Group DRUMS and add Glue Compressor with Ratio 4:1, Attack 3 ms, Release Auto, Threshold -5 dB, Soft Clip On.
steps.why: This exact kick placement gives the groove enough swing-like asymmetry to feel garage rather than straight house.
steps.tip: If the groove feels wrong, check the kick placements first. A single moved kick breaks a 2-step pattern quickly.

steps.title: Write the bass line
steps.instruction: On BASS load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Bass - 100%'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Create a 4-bar clip with these notes: F#1 at 1.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 92; C#2 at 1.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; F#1 at 1.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 90; A1 at 1.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; D1 at 2.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 89; A1 at 2.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; D1 at 2.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 87; F#1 at 2.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 70; F#1 at 3.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 92; C#2 at 3.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; F#1 at 3.3.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 90; A1 at 3.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; D1 at 4.1.1, length 2.5 beats, velocity 89; A1 at 4.3.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 70; E1 at 4.4.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 66.
steps.why: Alternating 1-bar harmony keeps the track moving. The last E1 is the exact lead-in that resets the phrase back into F# minor.
steps.tip: If the last E1 sounds odd alone, that is normal. It makes sense only because it points forward into the next bar.

steps.title: Process the bass
steps.instruction: After Serum add Saturator, Compressor, EQ Eight, and Utility. Saturator: Drive 4.5 dB, Soft Clip On, Output -2.7 dB. Compressor: Sidechain On, Audio From DRUMS, Threshold -18 dB, Ratio 4:1, Attack 0.30 ms, Release 80 ms. EQ Eight: High-Pass 30 Hz at 24 dB slope. Utility: Width 0 percent. Set BASS fader to -5.0 dB.
steps.why: The bass needs to stay quick enough for the 2-step pattern and still feel solid under the lead.
steps.tip: Do not lengthen the bass notes to make them feel warmer. Warmth in this track comes from pad and lead, not from a lazier low end.

steps.title: Load the pad and chord voicings
steps.instruction: On PAD load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Garage -> 'Pad- Samphire'. Leave it exactly as loaded. Your chords are F# minor = F#, A, C# and D major = D, F#, A.
steps.why: F# and A are shared between both chords, which is why the move feels warm and connected instead of dramatic.
steps.tip: This is a subtle lift progression. It should feel like light opening, not a big key change.

steps.title: Program the pad and return reverb
steps.instruction: Create a 4-bar PAD clip. Draw A3 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; C#4 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; F#4 at 1.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; A3 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; D4 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; F#4 at 2.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; A3 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; C#4 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; F#4 at 3.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64; A3 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 72; D4 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 68; F#4 at 4.1.1, length 3.5 beats, velocity 64. On VERB add Reverb with Decay Time 2.5 s, Pre-Delay 11 ms, Dry/Wet 100 percent, then EQ Eight High-Pass 220 Hz. On PAD add Compressor with Sidechain On, Audio From DRUMS, Threshold -22 dB, Ratio 4:1, Attack 0.10 ms, Release 126 ms. Set PAD send to VERB at -18.0 dB and PAD fader to -11.5 dB.
steps.why: Upper voicings keep the song airy. The sidechain makes the long pad sit inside the groove instead of on top of it.
steps.tip: These voicings are intentionally high. The lead needs room underneath them to sing.

steps.title: Write the lead and delay tail
steps.instruction: On LEAD load Serum 2 and open S1 Presets -> Leads -> 'LD Analogesque [EE]'. Leave the preset exactly as loaded. Create a 4-bar clip with these notes: C#5 at 1.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 82; A4 at 1.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 76; F#4 at 1.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; A4 at 2.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 80; F#4 at 2.3.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; D4 at 2.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72; C#5 at 3.1.1, length 1.0 beats, velocity 82; A4 at 3.2.3, length 0.5 beats, velocity 76; F#4 at 3.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; A4 at 4.1.1, length 1.5 beats, velocity 81; F#4 at 4.3.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 74; E4 at 4.4.1, length 0.5 beats, velocity 72. After Serum add Simple Delay with Time Left 1/8, Time Right 1/8, Feedback 14 percent, Dry/Wet 10 percent. Set LEAD send to VERB at -19.0 dB and LEAD fader to -10.0 dB.
steps.why: The lead is written to feel like it is glowing over the top of the groove. The small delay gives it a trailing ember effect without washing out the timing.
steps.tip: The last E4 is not part of the pad chord. It is there to pull you back into the next F# minor bar.

steps.title: Create the vocal pocket
steps.instruction: On PAD add EQ Eight after the Compressor: High-Pass 140 Hz, Bell 950 Hz at -3.5 dB with Q 1.50, Bell 2.5 kHz at -2.5 dB with Q 1.30. On LEAD add EQ Eight after Simple Delay: High-Pass 220 Hz, Bell 3.0 kHz at -2.0 dB with Q 1.20, High Shelf 8 kHz at -1.0 dB.
steps.why: This keeps the lead emotional without getting brittle and leaves a clean midrange hole for a future vocal if you ever add one.
steps.tip: If the lead still feels too sharp, reduce the shelf another 0.5 dB before touching the melody.

steps.title: Arrange the song with a warmer drop 2
steps.instruction: Build the arrangement exactly like this. Bars 1-8: intro, DRUMS only, and automate DRUMS group Utility Gain from -2 dB at bar 1 to 0 dB at bar 8. Bars 9-24: drop 1, DRUMS plus BASS plus PAD plus LEAD. Bars 25-32: break, PAD plus LEAD only, and automate VERB send on LEAD from -19 dB to -14 dB across the whole break. Delete all clips on bar 32 beat 4 for a 1-beat silence. Bars 33-48: drop 2A, full arrangement returns. Bars 49-64: drop 2B, duplicate the LEAD clip and raise clip Gain by +1.5 dB from bars 57-64 only, and automate PAD send to VERB from -18 dB to -15 dB across bars 49-64. Bars 65-72: outro, mute LEAD at bar 65, mute PAD at bar 69, mute BASS at bar 71, let DRUMS finish bar 72.
steps.why: The second drop gets warmer and wider through exact level and send changes. That is the lesson: big feeling from precise moves, not from chaos.
steps.tip: The break should feel suspended, not empty. If it feels empty, the lead send automation is probably too low.

steps.title: Set the mix
steps.instruction: Set DRUMS group to -4.0 dB, BASS to -5.0 dB, PAD to -11.5 dB, LEAD to -10.0 dB, VERB return to -18.5 dB, and Master to 0.0 dB. Add Limiter on Master with Ceiling -0.30 dB.
steps.why: These levels keep the 2-step groove tight while still giving the lead enough emotional weight to carry the song.
steps.tip: Judge the whole mix from bars 57-64. That is where the arrangement is richest.

steps.title: Export Slow Ember
steps.instruction: Export bars 1 through 72 as WAV, 24-bit, 44.1 kHz with filename 'original-13-slow-ember-v1.wav'. After export, compare bars 9-24 and 49-64 and confirm the second drop feels warmer and more open because of automation, not because the song lost clarity.
steps.why: That comparison is the whole artistic lesson of the track.
steps.tip: If the second drop feels messy instead of warm, reduce the duplicated lead clip gain first.
